description: Protein structure and function rules for therapeutic design - domains, active sites, binding interfaces, and druggability.

facts.context: Enzyme active sites for small molecule drug targeting typically:
facts.truth: are conserved pockets where catalysis occurs, often with key residues for binding
distractors: are located on the protein surface randomly
cannot be targeted by small molecules

facts.context: Allosteric drug binding sites differ from active sites because they:
facts.truth: modulate activity by inducing conformational changes at a distance from active site
distractors: are always more potent than orthosteric sites
are identical to the active site

facts.context: Protein-protein interaction (PPI) interfaces are challenging drug targets because:
facts.truth: they are typically flat, large (1500-3000 A²), and lack deep pockets
distractors: PPIs have deep active sites like enzymes

facts.context: PPI hot spots are residues that contribute disproportionately to binding. They are typically:
facts.truth: Trp, Tyr, Arg residues that contribute >2 kcal/mol to binding free energy

facts.context: ATP-competitive kinase inhibitors typically bind to:
facts.truth: the hinge region connecting N and C lobes, mimicking adenine interactions
distractors: sites far from the ATP pocket

facts.context: The DFG motif in kinases determines inhibitor type. Type II inhibitors bind when DFG is:
facts.truth: in DFG-out conformation, accessing hydrophobic pocket behind ATP site

facts.context: GPCR orthosteric binding sites for small molecule drugs are located:
facts.truth: within transmembrane helical bundle, accessible from extracellular side

facts.context: Antibody binding specificity is determined by:
facts.truth: CDR loops (complementarity determining regions) in variable domains

facts.context: Protein thermal stability (Tm) affects drug development because:
facts.truth: higher Tm correlates with longer shelf life and resistance to aggregation
distractors: Tm has no effect on manufacturability
lower Tm proteins are always preferred

facts.context: Therapeutic protein aggregation is problematic because aggregates can:
facts.truth: be immunogenic, reduce efficacy, and cause injection site reactions
stabilize structure but require oxidizing conditions for correct formation
distractors: are never present in secreted proteins

facts.context: N-linked glycosylation of therapeutic antibodies affects:
